description: Deterministic grounding-gate oracle for the neutral acme-notes product. A fixed candidate note-set over a known closed span set, and exactly which artifacts survive: valid kept, one out-of-set citation pruned (artifact kept with its remaining in-set evidence), empty/ungrounded dropped, and one mention grounded-but-never-persisted. The acme-notes e2e loads this to drive the deterministic extractor and to assert the served read model. Drives the REAL open-core enforcers only (closedReferenceGroundingChecker set-membership + on_empty_evidence: drop + grounded-not-persisted) — there is NO verbatim-quote or attribution validator in open-core, so no such field is load-bearing.

segments.text: We selected the second option for the layout.
That estimate looks optimistic to me.

candidate_notes.headline: Layout notes captured.
candidate_notes.detail: The session settled the layout option and named an owner for the export.

expected_dropped.reason: all cited spans (system:s9) are outside the closed set -> pruned to empty -> dropped

notes: Span ids derive from segments order as `${track}:s${index}` (the STT normalizer).

An item survives only if at least one cited span is in the closed span set (closedReferenceGroundingChecker); out-of-set citations are pruned, and an evidence-required member pruned to empty is dropped (on_empty_evidence: drop).
The digest is evidence-exempt (provenance.required: false) — a projection member that always persists with empty evidence.
Mentions are grounded but NEVER flattened into note_artifacts (no artifact kind references them).
